ground_truth.title: Online and Remote Learning: Tips for Students – The Learning Center
ground_truth.main_content:
Consider the following tips and strategies as you navigate online and remote classes at UNC. Adapt these suggestions to best suit how you learn and work optimally.

Healthy mindset: Shifting to an online work model can be a tough time for all of us. Here are some resources for when mental blocks come up. If you are worried about procrastination, see this handout and video on taming procrastination. If you notice a line of thinking that's getting in the way of your work, check out our handout on Self-Scripting. Here's a handy handout on staying motivated.

Routines: When our day-to-day, external structures are changed or removed, most of us benefit from implementing our own structured schedule. How will you create this for yourself? Wake up and go to bed at about the same time daily. Try alarms to keep you on schedule. Mark when you need to be present for real-time online classes, for instructor-led review sessions, or for study groups with peers. Mark your calendar with appointments for independent studying, reading, and assignment completion for each class.

Study space: Earmarking a space dedicated for study will help you stay organized and productive. What will your study space look like? Explore if you work best with quiet or ambient noise. Try turning off your phone for set periods of time. Internet blocking sites can help you stay away from time-wasters online. Try separate folders for each class—both digital and hard-copy.

Setting goals: Starting each day and each week with clear and concrete goals will give your days purpose and structure. Any large papers or projects due at the end of the semester? Break down projects into a series of discrete steps, and schedule these steps into your calendar. Try working backwards from the due date.

Practice regular communication: Communication will be critical to stay connected to all parts of your academic life while you're away from campus. Are you familiar with how each of your professors use Canvas? What new features in Canvas might your professor use with online instruction?

Practice self care: Just like when you're on campus, it will still be important to take good care of yourself so that you can be at your best. If you have an exercise regimen, how might it need to be adapted? What will you do to manage stress and anxiety? What will you do to ensure adequate sleep and good nutrition?
